::ab[Secrets of the Grave (Ex)]{icon=boost l=2 passive="A dirge bard gains a bonus equal to half his bard level on Knowledge (religion) checks made to identify undead creatures and their abilities." ability="A dirge bard may use mind-affecting spells to affect undead as if they were living creatures, even if they are mindless (though spells that affect only humanoids do not affect them, even if they were humanoids in life)." order=pa special="In addition, he may add one ‹main/spells_>necromancy« spell› from the spell list of any arcane spellcasting class to his list of spells known at 2nd level and every four levels thereafter (6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels)." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Haunting Refrain (Su)]{l=5 icon=lower ability="A dirge bard is able to stir primal terrors in the hearts of listeners. He can use a Perform (keyboard) or Perform (percussion) check in place of an Intimidate check to demoralize an opponent, with a bonus equal to half his bard level. In addition, saving throws against any fear effect he creates are made with a -2 penalty." imp10="The penalty becomes -3." imp15="The penalty becomes -4." imp20="The penalty becomes -5." replace="Lore master"}

disciple_of_the_forked_tongue.name: Disciple of the Forked Tongue
description: ## Disciple of the Forked Tongue
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/7›

Studied in venomous words as well as venomous blood, a disciple of the forked tongue knows just the right phrase to slowly poison the soul. His voice can sink deep into his victims' hearts, destroying friendships and devouring the mind.
::prereq{r=Vishkanya}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A disciple of the forked tongue gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Discordant Spiral (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="The disciple can disrupt the thought processes of enemies who hear his performance, causing them to take a -1 penalty on saves against mind-affecting and curse effects and a -2 penalty on ‹rule/concentration› checks." imp5="These penalties become -2 and -3, respectively." imp11="These penalties become -3 and -4." imp17="These penalties become -4 and -5." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Venomous Whispers (Su)]{l=9 icon=lower ability="A disciple of the forked tongue can use his performance to sow distrust into the mind of an enemy within 30 feet. An affected creature treats its allies as hostile for the purposes of their spells and abilities - it is never considered a willing target of spells and must attempt saving throws against them when possible, and it gains no benefit from allied bardic performances and other abilities that specifically target allies." imp12="He can now affect two creatures at the same time." imp15="He can now affect three creatures at the same time." imp18="He can now affect four creatures at the same time." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Serpent of the Mind (Su)]{icon=learn l2="A disciple of the forked tongue can add one spell with the ‹spelldef/curse› descriptor from the spell list of any spellcasting class to his list of spells known." l6="He adds another *curse* spell to his list of spells known." l10="He adds another *curse* spell." l14="He adds another *curse* spell." l18="He adds another *curse* spell." replace="Versatile performance"}

dragon_herald.name: Dragon Herald
description: ## Dragon Herald
‹SOURCE Kobolds of Golarion/22›

Dragon heralds are messengers and evangelists of true dragonkind. They carry the word of their dragon patrons far and wide, whether that's a message of peace or a declaration of war. A kobold whose scales already associate her with a revered dragon is often trained as her tribe's dragon herald.
::prereq{r=Kobold}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down ability="The dragon herald is proficient with simple weapons and light armor, but not with shields. Dragon heralds are messengers, not warriors. Like other bards, dragon heralds don't incur the normal arcane spell failure chance from wearing light armor."}

::ab[Dragon Patron (Ex)]{icon=info choice="A dragon herald must choose a particular ‹family/true dragon› species whose glory to extol. In most cases, this is a dragon with whom the herald is familiar, but it could be a dragon deity, a long-dead dragon hero, or even an entire dragon lineage. The energy type associated with her dragon patron (as shown on the ‹sorcbloodline/draconic« sorcerer bloodline›) determines the energy type used by several of her abilities. She must also designate a sacred space relevant to her dragon choice - usually the lair of a patron dragon or a shrine or holy place associated with a dragon type."}

::ab[Coat of Arms (Ex)]{icon=def passive="A dragon herald gains ‹umr/energy »resistance› equal to her dragon herald level against the energy type associated with her dragon patron. This resistance stacks with any inherent racial resistances, but not with magical or temporary resistances."}

::ab[Dragon Voice (Ex)]{icon=boost-power passive="A dragon herald gains a bonus equal to half her level on Intimidate and Diplomacy checks. She gains Draconic as a bonus language." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A dragon herald gains the following types of bardic performance:"}

::ab[Diplomatic Immunity (Su)]{next icon=def ability="A dragon herald can use bardic performance to generate a ‹spell/sanctuary› effect upon herself. Most use this to deliver the word of their patron or to safely complete Diplomacy or Intimidate checks in the presence of hostile foes. The effect persists for as long as the dragon herald performs and for 1 round after." replace=Countersong~fascinate}

::ab[Diplomatic Protection (Su)]{next l=3 icon=protect ability="The dragon herald can use bardic performance to wrap a single ally in the colors and scales of her patron. The dragon herald must be able to see her ally, and that ally must be able to perceive her performance. While the herald performs, the target gains resistance equal to twice the dragon herald's level to the energy type associated with the dragon herald's patron, and an enhancement bonus to natural armor equal to 1/2 the dragon herald's level." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Rebuke Foes (Su)]{next l=12 icon=zap ability="The dragon herald gains the ability to rain down her patron's wrath on her enemies, dealing energy damage of the appropriate type equal to twice her dragon herald level (Reflex save for half). She can target one enemy within 50 feet for every 4 dragon herald levels she has. This effect occurs once per round for as long as she continues her performance." replace="Soothing performance"}

::ab[Retreat to Lair (Sp)]{next l=15 icon=magic fullround="The dragon herald can spend 5 daily uses of performance to teleport herself, or a willing target touched, to her sacred place." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Master of Persuasion (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="The dragon herald becomes so sure in her craft that she can take 10 on any Diplomacy or Intimidate check, even when rushed or threatened." order=ar fullround="Once per day, instead of spending a full minute, she may attempt either a Diplomacy or Intimidate check." imp11="You can use the full-round ability twice a day." imp17="You can use the full-round ability three times a day." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Extol Glory (Su)]{l=10 icon=power passive="The dragon herald gains the ability to spread her patron's glory to all intelligent creatures. When she speaks Draconic, any intelligent creature that hears her and knows any language can understand her words." special="This ability does not allow the dragon herald to understand languages with which she is not familiar." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}

dragon_yapper.name: Dragon Yapper
description: ## Dragon Yapper
‹SOURCE Monster Codex/128›
Kobolds' yammering songs distract opponents in combat, hindering their ability to attack.
::prereq{r=Kobold}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A dragon yapper gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Yapping Song (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A dragon yapper can use performance to annoy those that hear it, causing them to take a -1 penalty on attack and damage rolls (minimum 1) and a -1 penalty on saves against fear effects and charm effects as long as the dragon yapper continues performing." imp5="These penalties become -2." imp11="These penalties become -3." imp17="These penalties become -4." special="Yapping song is a mind-affecting ability that uses audible components, but is not language-dependent." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Frightful Song (Su)]{next l=8 icon=lower-aura ability="A dragon yapper can amplify his voice to sound like an actual dragon. Enemies within 30 feet become ‹misc/shaken› unless they succeed at a Will save (DC 10 +1/2 the dragon yapper's level + the dragon yapper's Charisma modifier). A successful save renders targets immune to this ability for 24 hours." special="Frightful song is a mind-affecting ability, and a fear effect that uses audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}
::ab[Versatile Performance (Ex)]{icon=info l2="A dragon yapper must choose sing at 2nd level."}

duettist.name: Duettist
description: ## Duettist
‹SOURCE Familiar Folio/6›

Whether singing a delicate duo with a nightingale or slipping coins from purses while a trained monkey distracts the crowd, the duettist blends his bond to his familiar with his natural talent for performance to create amazing effects.
::ab[Diminished Competency]{icon=down note="A duettist does not gain the *well-versed* or *jack-of-all-trades* class features."}
::ab[Familiar (Ex)]{icon=power l1="A duettist gains a ‹sidekick/familiar›, treating his bard level as his effective wizard level for the purpose of this ability." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Versatile Familiar (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="A duettist's familiar learns to use performance to supplement its skills." l2="A duettist's familiar benefits from its master's *versatile performance* class feature." alter="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Performing Familiar (Su)]{icon=power flavor="A duettist's familiar learns how to create supernatural effects with its performances, just like its master." l4="The familiar can use any of its master's bardic performances, but only the familiar or the duettist can have a performance active at any given time, not both. If one is performing and the other starts a performance, the previous performance immediately ends." special="Each round that the familiar performs consumes 2 rounds of the duettist's bardic performance." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Harmonizing Familiar (Su)]{icon=boost flavor="The duettist and his familiar have learned how to perform together in harmony." l8="When a duettist and his familiar perform the same bardic performance simultaneously, its effects are enhanced. If the performance has a DC, the DC increases by 2. If the performance provides a competence bonus, the competence bonus increases by 1." special="Because both the duettist and the familiar are performing, each round performed consumes 3 rounds of bardic performance." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Symphonic Familiar (Su)]{icon=power flavor="The duettist and his familiar have learned how to create a symphony of complementary performances that meld together to produce two different effects." l14="They can each perform a different bardic performance simultaneously, and each has its full effect." special="Because both the duettist and the familiar are performing, each round performed consumes 3 rounds of bardic performance." replace="Frightening tune"}

dwarven_scholar.name: Dwarven Scholar
description: ## Dwarven Scholar
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/30›
A dwarven scholar researches the lineage of the kings of the old dwarven empires and learns their ancient tactics. She keeps these ancient traditions alive by sharing them with her allies.
::prereq{other="This archetype appears to be intended for ‹race/dwarves› only, but that limitation is never explicitly stated."}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down note="A dwarven scholar is proficient with one weapon of her choosing with the word &quot&dwarven&quot& in its name, in addition to her normal weapon proficiencies. She is not proficient with shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Studied Insight (Ex)]{icon=info note="A dwarven scholar uses her Wisdom modifier instead of her Charisma modifier to determine all class features and effects relating to her bard class, including her bardic performance ability, bonus spells per day, the maximum spell level she can cast, and the save DCs of her spells."}

::ab[Dwarven Training (Ex)]{icon=power l2="A dwarven scholar masters one aspect of dwarven combat and gains a bonus ‹main/combat feat›. She must meet the prerequisites for this feat, but she treats her bard level as her base attack bonus (in addition to base attack bonuses gained from other classes and racial Hit Dice) for the purpose of qualifying for this feat." l6="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." l10="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." l14="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." l18="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A dwarven scholar gains the following type of bardic performance."}

::ab[War Chant (Su)]{next icon=boost l1="A dwarven scholar can use her performance to inspire the martial prowess of the ancient dwarves in her allies. The dwarven scholar selects a single combat feat she knows. Allies affected by this performance gain the benefit of this feat for as long as the performance is maintained. To be affected, an ally must be able to perceive the dwarven scholar's performance. If a combat feat has a daily use limitation (such as ‹feat/Stunning Fist›), any uses of that combat feat by the dwarven scholar's allies while using this ability counts toward the dwarven scholar's feat's daily limit." l6="The dwarven scholar may grant the benefit of two combat feats at the same time. She may use one of these feats to meet a prerequisite of the second feat. Maintaining two feats in this way requires spending 2 rounds of bardic performance every round." replace="Inspire courage~suggestion"}

faith_singer.name: Faith Singer
description: ## Faith Singer
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/19›
Faith singers show their faith in their deity with songs or other appropriate performances.
::ab[Faithful]{icon=info note="Like a cleric, a faith singer must worship a ‹main/deity»<faiths›, and his alignment must be within one step of his deity's alignment."}

::ab[Devout Spell Knowledge (Su)]{icon=power-magic l2="A faith singer can select one ‹ability/domain<s› from among those belonging to his deity. Once per day as a spell-like ability using his bard level as his caster level, he can cast the 1st-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l6="He can similarly cast the 2nd-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l10="He can similarly cast the 3rd-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l14="He can similarly cast the 4th-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." l18="He can similarly cast the 5th-level domain spell while giving a bardic performance." special="He can use each spell-like ability only once per day." replace="Versatile performance"}

fey_courtier.name: Fey Courtier
description: ## Fey Courtier
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/11›
Fey who associate with courts often become bards skilled at navigating the inhuman societies of the trackless wilds.
::prereq{r=Gathlain}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="The fey courtier has access to the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Scorn of the Wilds (Sp)]{next l=8 icon=magic ability="The fey courtier can use bardic performance to permanently mark a creature with nature's displeasure by spending 2 rounds performing while the target remains within 30 feet. The performance includes a litany of the target's misdeeds against fey and nature. Unless the target succeeds at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half the bard's level + his Charisma modifier), it is cursed as per ‹spell/nature's exile› or ‹spell/bestow curse›." imp14="The fey courtier's options expand to include ‹spell/baleful polymorph› and ‹spell/green caress›." special="As part of the performance, the fey courtier must define a condition under which the curse will be lifted, which must relate to making amends for the crime against fey or nature and cannot be suicidal to carry out. A creature that succeeds at its saving throw is immune to the fey courtier's scorn of the wild for 24 hours. This is a curse effect." replace="Dirge of doom~frightening tune"}

::ab[Stone Dance (Sp)]{next l=15 icon=magic ability="The bard's performance can move even the features of the natural world. This performance functions as ‹spell/animate plants› (DC = 10 + half the bard's level + his Charisma modifier) except it can also animate unworked stone and water from natural bodies of water (with statistics equivalent to an appropriate plant)." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Fey Contacts]{l=2 icon=power-boost passive="Thanks to his fey contacts and knowledge of the byzantine trade customs of the fey courts, a fey courtier can treat any wilderness area with fey residents as a settlement with a certain base value and purchase limit based on his bard level for the purpose of buying and selling magic items (see the table below). The fey courtier can also use these contacts to gather information about the residents, geography, and nearby areas with Diplomacy." replace="Versatile performance"}
These rules come from ‹rule/The Settlement Stat Block›:

>*There is a 75% chance that any item of [the Base Value] or lower can be found for sale in the community with little effort. If an item is not available, a new check to determine if the item has become available can be made in 1 week. A settlement's purchase limit is the most money a shop in the settlement can spend to purchase any single item from the PCs. If the PCs wish to sell an item worth more than a settlement's purchase limit, they'll either need to settle for a lower price, travel to a larger city, or (with the GM's permission) search for a specific buyer in the city with deeper pockets.*
| Bard Level | Base Value | Purchase Limit |

::ab[Summon Fey Allies]{l=3 icon=learn-power ability="The fey courtier gains ‹feat/Augment Summoning› as a bonus feat and can call upon natural allies who owe him favors. He adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› to his list of 1st-level bard spells known. Whenever he gains access to another spell level, he adds the next-higher *summon nature's ally* spell to his list of bard spells known." special="The fey courtier adds the following creatures to the *summon nature's ally* list: 1st-‹monster/grimple« (gremlin)›, ‹monster/mite›, ‹monster/sprite›; 2nd-‹monster/atomie›, ‹monster/fuath« (gremlin)›, ‹monster/nixie›; 3rd-‹monster/leprechaun›, ‹monster/nuglub« (gremlin)›, ‹monster/pooka›; 4th-‹monster/kelpie›, ‹monster/korred›, ‹monster/twigjack›; 5th-‹monster/lampad›, ‹monster/lurker in light›, ‹monster/swan maiden›; 6th-‹monster/cold rider›, ‹monster/oceanid›, ‹monster/sangoi›." replace="Inspire competence"}

fey_prankster.name: Fey Prankster
description: ## Fey Prankster
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/20›
Sly and mischievous like the Lantern King himself, fey pranksters learn supernatural tricks that can cause their victims to suffer mishaps and accidents.

::ab[Mischievous Talent (Ex)]{icon=boost-power passive="A fey prankster adds half her class level (minimum 1) on Bluff, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth skill checks, and can attempt Sleight of Hand checks untrained." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A fey prankster gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Song of Clumsiness (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A fey prankster can use this performance to cause enemies within 30 feet that can hear her to suffer seemingly random mishaps. Any enemy in this area that draws a weapon or retrieves an item from a backpack or similar container immediately drops it, and falls prone the first time it enters a square of ‹rule/difficult terrain› during its turn. A creature can negate a specific effect with a successful Reflex saving throw (DC = 10 + half the fey prankster's bard level + her Charisma modifier), but an enemy must attempt a separate save for each potential effect during the song of clumsiness (for example, if it draws multiple weapons)." special="Song of clumsiness is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting ability that relies on audible components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Incite Unreliability (Su)]{next icon=lower ability="A fey prankster can use her performance to manipulate a single target into fighting erratically or haphazardly, as though affected by ‹spell/lesser confusion›. To be affected, the target must be within 30 feet and able to hear the fey prankster's performance. The effect persists for as long as the target can hear the performance. A target that succeeds at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half the fey prankster's bard level + her Charisma modifier) negates the effect and becomes immune to incite unreliability from the same fey prankster for 24 hours." special="Incite unreliability is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting ability that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Embarrassing Satire (Su)]{next icon=zap l=8 ability="A fey prankster's powerful satire raises painful blisters and ugly boils on the face of her target. The fey prankster selects one target within 30 feet to become ‹misc/sickened› from the uncomfortable facial boils. The sickened effect continues for as long as the target is within 30 feet and the fey prankster continues the performance, though the boils remain for 1 day afterward." special="Embarrassing satire is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that relies on audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Dirty Trickster (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A fey prankster gains ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat and counts as having ‹feat/Combat Expertise› for the purpose of meeting the prerequisites of feats that have Improved Dirty Trick as a prerequisite." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Master of Mischief (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost ability="A fey prankster can take 10 on a Bluff, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth skill check (if she has ranks in that skill), even if distracted or endangered. In addition, once per day, the fey prankster can take 20 on one these skill checks (if she has ranks in that skill)." useInc="6~bard~5" replace="Lore master"}

filidh.name: Filidh
description: ## Filidh
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/40›
By tapping into the world's natural music, specialized bards known as filidhs are able to see not only the tapestry of life but divine portents of the future from the rhythm of all life's song.

::ab[Armor and Weapon Proficiency]{icon=down note="A filidh is proficient with light armor and is prohibited from wearing metal armor. A filidh can wear wooden armor that has been altered by the ‹spell/ironwood› spell. Filidhs are proficient with shields (except tower shields) but can use only those made of wood." special="A filidh who wears prohibited armor or uses a prohibited shield is unable to cast bard spells or use any of his supernatural or spell-like class abilities while doing so and for 24 hours thereafter." alter="Armor and weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Natural Magic]{icon=magic note="A filidh casts spells as a bard, but the spells are divine, not arcane, and therefore not subject to arcane spell failure. A filidh must use a holy symbol or a musical instrument as a divine focus when a spell includes such a component. He still uses the bard spell list." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Nature's Song]{icon=magic useL=bard useUnit=time ability="Filidhs can hear the resonant song generated by all living creatures - a primal music that stretches back to the beginning of time and ahead to the unwritten future. By tapping into this cosmic melody, a filidh can steal glimpses of the future." order=awu swift="A filidh can sacrifice a spell slot to gain an additional number of rounds of bardic performance for that day equal to the spell's level."}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A filidh gains the following types of bardic performances."}

::ab[Echoes of Nature's Song (Su)]{next icon=protect ability="A filidh can use his bardic performance to imbue his allies with subconscious knowledge of their own futures, improving their reflexes and ability to avoid danger. An affected ally receives a +1 insight bonus on Reflex saving throws and to AC." imp5="This bonus becomes +2." imp11="This bonus becomes +3." imp17="This bonus becomes +4." special="This performance is a mind-affecting ability and relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Divinatory Song (Sp)]{next l=6 icon=magic ability="A filidh can use his performance to create an effect equivalent to ‹spell/divination›, using the filidh's level as the caster level. The filidh and all allies who can hear his performance receive the information provided by the divination as flashes of inspiration and knowledge." special="Divinatory song takes 10 minutes and uses 6 rounds of bardic performance. Divinatory song relies on audible components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Voices of Life (Su)]{next l=8 icon=magic ability="A filidh can grant himself and all allies who can hear his performance the ability to speak with animals and plants as if affected by ‹spell/speak with animals› and ‹spell/speak with plants› so long as he maintains this performance." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Unity of Life (Su)]{next icon=magic-protect l=15 ability="A filidh can use his performance to interlink the life force of two allies who can hear his performance as though they were affected by ‹spell/shield other›, using the filidh's bard level as his caster level. The filidh designates which ally is the warded target and which ally receives half the warded creature's damage." order=af free="The filidh can switch the targets of this ability (and the effect of the performance the targets receive) once per round at the start of each turn that he maintains the performance." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Song of the Cycle (Su)]{next l=20 icon=protect ability="A filidh can grant awe-inspiring glimpses into the future with his magic. All allies who can see and hear the filidh are affected as though by the personal version of ‹spell/foresight› for the duration of the performance." replace="Deadly performance"}

first_world_minstrel.name: First World Minstrel
description: ## First World Minstrel
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/11›

Most cultures tell stories of people stolen away by the fey, only to return years later, forever changed. The First World minstrel was changed in such a way by the fey, and his performances channel the mysterious powers of the First World.
::ab[Fey Magic]{icon=learn note="A First World minstrel adds ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spells to his spell list instead of the equivalent ‹spell/summon monster› spells." alter="Spell list"}

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A First World minstrel gains the ‹ability/wild empathy› druid class feature, using his bard level as his druid level for this ability." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A First World minstrel gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Echoes of the First World (Su)]{next icon=power ability="A First World minstrel infuses himself or a single willing ally within 30 feet with the strange magic of the First World, granting the target one of the special abilities from the ‹template/fey creature« template› other than *change shape* for 1 round." special="For every 3 levels beyond 1st the First World minstrel has, he can target one additional ally or add an additional special ability to one of his targets. This is a polymorph effect that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Gremlin's Luck (Su)]{next icon=lower-roll ability="A First World minstrel can use his performance to inflict bad luck on a target within 30 feet for 1 round. Whenever the target rolls an ability check, attack roll, saving throw, or skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result. A target that succeeds at a Will save (DC 10 + half the First World minstrel's level + his Charisma modifier) negates the effect and is immune to this ability for 24 hours." special="This is a mind-affecting effect that relies on audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}
::ab[Resist Nature's Lure (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A First World minstrel gains the ‹ability/resist nature's lure› druid class feature." replace=Well-versed}

flame_dancer.name: Flame Dancer
description: ## Flame Dancer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/81›

A flame dancer studies the movements of fire, adding its grace to his repertoire. He seeks truth in fire's burning essence, and uses his performance to unleash the power of fire against those who dare oppose him.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A flame dancer gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Fire Dance (Su)]{next icon=def-protect l=1 flavor="A fire dancer learns to protect himself and others from the pain of fire, and discovers how to control the flame while enduring its heat." ability="Each round of the *fire dance,* he rolls a Perform (dance or sing) check. Any ally within 30 feet of the bard that has caught on fire or is affected by a fire effect or extreme heat can use the bard's Perform check result in place of its saving throw against that fire. Any ally within 30 feet of the bard who is suffering from ‹rule/heat«stroke› can ignore the fatigue from heat exposure so long as the bard maintains this performance." special="*Fire dance* relies on audible or visual components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Song of the Fiery Gaze (Su)]{next l=3 icon=power flavor="A fire dancer can allow allies to see through flames without any distortion. Any ally within 30 feet of the bard who can hear the performance can see through fire, fog, and smoke without penalty as long as the light is sufficient to allow him to see normally, as with the base effect of the ‹mystery/gaze of »flame«s› oracle revelation." special="*Song of the fiery gaze* relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Fire Break (Su)]{next l=6 icon=protect flavor="A fire dancer's performance can bend flames away from others." ability="Any ally within 30 feet of the bard who can hear or see the bardic performance gains ‹umr/resist› fire 20 as long as the performance is maintained." imp11="This resistance increases to 30." special="*Fire break* relies on audible or visual components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Fan the Flames (Su)]{icon=learn l=8 note="A fire dancer adds ‹spell/burning hands›, ‹spell/flaming sphere›, and ‹spell/fireball› to his list of bard spells known (as 1st-, 2nd-, and 3rd-level spells, respectively)." replace="Dirge of doom"}

flamesinger.name: Flamesinger
description: ## Flamesinger
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/5›

The flamesinger finds inspiration in the mesmerizing beauty of fire. While the flamesinger's tradition originated among the varied peoples of the Padishah Empire of Kelesh, it long ago spread to Qadira and from there throughout the Inner Sea region.

::ab[Fire Music]{icon=power-learn l1="A flamesinger gains ‹feat/Fire Music› as a bonus feat (without needing the meet the normal prerequisites)." l4="When she gains access to 2nd-level spells (4th level), she adds ‹spell/summon monster I› and ‹spell/summon monster II› to her bard spells known; every 3 levels thereafter (7th, 10th, 13th, 16th) she gains the next level of *summon monster,* until she adds ‹spell/summon monster VI› to her spells known at 16th level." special="The flamesinger must apply the Fire Music feat's effect when casting these spells." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A flamesinger gains the following bardic performance."}

::ab[Blazing Blades (Su)]{next icon=boost-magic ability="The flamesinger's song causes allies' weapons to burst into flames. To be affected, an ally must be able to hear the bard perform and be within 30 feet. An affected creature's successful attacks with manufactured or natural weapons deal 1d4 additional points of fire damage. These flames do not harm the weapon or its wielder. This damage stacks with other sources of fire damage, such as the ‹magic-enh/flaming› special weapon ability." imp5="The bonus damage becomes 2d4." imp11="The bonus damage becomes 3d4." imp17="The bonus damage becomes 4d4." special="This is a fire effect that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Wildfire (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="Like a raging wildfire, a flamesinger moves with blistering speed." l2="The flamesinger gains a +5-foot enhancement bonus to her base speed." l6="The bonus increases to +10 feet." l10="The bonus increases to +15 feet." l14="The bonus increases to +20 feet." l18="The bonus increases to +25 feet." replace="Versatile performance"}

fortune_teller.name: Fortune-Teller
description: ## Fortune-Teller
‹SOURCE Villain Codex/32›

Instead of using song and dance, a fortune-teller influences people by divining their fate. Though he learns a great deal about the paths of destiny, his true skill is in making others think he knows more than he truly does.

::ab[Fortune-Teller's Divinations]{icon=learn note="Add the following spells to the fortune-teller's bard spell list. He doesn't automatically gain them and must still select them as spells known if he wants to use them. 0-‹spell/guidance›; 1st-‹spell/object reading›; 2nd-‹spell/augury›; 3rd-‹spell/analyze aura›; 4th-‹spell/divination›; 5th-‹spell/retrocognition›; 6th-‹spell/true seeing›, ‹spell/vision›." alter="Spell list"}

:::ab{flavor="A fortune-teller can use Perform (oratory) to read a fortune as part of a bardic performance." icon=magic ability=Ability title="Oracular Performance" alter="Bardic performance" replace=Countersong~distraction}

Once per hour when the bard performs a fortune-telling using a fortune-teller's deck, a crystal ball, or a similar item, he can read the fortune of one subject of the bardic performance. This is part of the same action used to activate the performance. The fortune-teller rolls d%, adds his bard level to the result, and consults the list below. The outcome depends on whether the chosen creature is an ally (for a beneficial performance) or an enemy (for a detrimental performance).
- Ally: **Woe**
- Enemy: **Weal**
- **Inconclusive**
- Ally: **Weal**
- Enemy: **Woe**

On an inconclusive result, the bard fails to get any information from the reading and the subject is affected by the performance normally. For an ally, on a weal result the subject's bonus from the performance increases by 1, and on a woe result the subject's bonus decreases by 1 (minimum 0). This affects only numeric bonuses. For example, a subject wouldn't gain another Hit Die from *inspire greatness.*

For an enemy, on a weal result the subject gains a +2 bonus on its saving throw to negate the bard's performance, and on a woe result it takes a -2 penalty on its save. An oracular performance has no effect on an enemy if the performance doesn't require a saving throw.

All adjustments from an oracular performance on a foe last only for that instance of the bardic performance. All adjustments on an ally last for 1 minute, applying each time the fortune-teller uses that particular bardic performance on that ally but ending when the duration expires, even if the performance continues.

::ab[Fortune-Teller's Acumen]{l=2 icon=magic ability="Whenever he casts a spell from the divination school, a fortune-teller can use a fortune-teller's deck, crystal ball, or similar item instead of the spell's material component, as long as the cost of the material component is no more than 100 gp per bard level. If he chooses to cast the spell using such an item and the spell's normal material component (regardless of that component's cost), he casts the spell at caster level." special="This special focus can't be used with other special foci that have a similar effect, but can be used with spells that normally require a focus component (which must also be provided)." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A fortune-teller gains the following bardic performance."}

:::ab{next title="Transparent Fate (Su)" l=8 icon=lower-aura ability=Ability special="A creature that was targeted by oracular performance when this performance began applies any save DC adjustment to this secondary save as well. At the GM's discretion, the revelation of the creature's fate might have other consequences; for example, a creature's allies might see it was planning to betray them." replace="Dirge of doom"}

The fortune-teller can cause all enemies within 30 feet to have their near future revealed for all to see. A successful Will saving throw (DC = 10 1/2 the fortune-teller's level the fortune-teller's Cha modifier) negates the effect. This ability causes any creature attacked by an affected enemy to gain a +2 bonus to AC and on saving throws against the attack.

As a secondary effect, if an affected creature's hit points are reduced to less than its Constitution score, it also sees a vision of its own death and must succeed at another Will save or become frightened for 1 round. A given creature needs to save against this secondary effect at most once per performance of transparent fate, even if its hit points are reduced to less than its Constitution score multiple times.

geisha.name: Geisha
description: ## Geisha
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/27›

In some cultures, the professional entertainer is a prestigious role. Specially trained entertainers called geisha are praised for their appearance and skill at conversation, music, dancing, singing, poetry, and calligraphy. A geisha provides social intimacy and status but not physical intimacy.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-magic note="Geisha are proficient in all simple weapons and one ‹class/monk› weapon. Geisha are not proficient in any armor or shield; unlike bards, geisha are subject to arcane spell failure even when casting in light armor or when using a shield." alter="Armor and weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Tea Ceremony (Su)]{icon=boost ability="By spending 10 minutes preparing an elaborate tea ceremony, a geisha may affect her allies with *inspire courage, inspire competence, inspire greatness,* or *inspire heroics.* The ceremony's effects last 10 minutes. The geisha must spend 4 rounds of bardic performance for each creature to be affected."}

::ab[Geisha Knowledge]{icon=boost passive="A geisha adds half her class level (minimum 1) on Craft (calligraphy) checks, Diplomacy checks, Knowledge (nobility) checks, and one type of Perform check (act, dance, oratory, percussion, string, or sing); she may make checks with these skills untrained." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Scribe Scroll]{icon=power ability="A geisha gains ‹feat/Scribe Scroll› as a bonus feat."}

hatharat_agent.name: Hatharat Agent
description: ## Hatharat Agent
‹SOURCE Qadira, Jewel of the East/47›
Although the Hatharat employs all manner of specialists, bards who take up the role of agent are among its most infamous and widespread.

:::ab[Called Favor (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability=Ability title="Called Favor (Ex)" replace="Bardic knowledge" imp7="An agent need only wait 1d6 days between uses of called favors. In addition to the favors above, he can now ask for a favor that supplies the aid of a single 5th-level specialist for 24 hours. This specialist is treated as a short-term ‹feat/cohort»<leadership› for the agent, but should remain under the control of the GM. The exact statistics for the specialist (who can be of any class) should also be calculated by the GM." imp9="The specialist can now be 6th-level." imp11="The specialist can now be 7th-level." imp13="The specialist can now be 8th-level." imp15="The specialist can now be 9th-level." imp17="The specialist can now be 10th-level." imp18="The specialist can now be 11th-level." special="You can find numerous pregenerated NPCs via the links at the bottom of the ‹main/Monsters« and NPCs page›."}

A Hatharat agent can use his contacts to ask for a favor from various individuals who have benefited from Hatharat aid in the past. The agent must spend 1d4 hours in an urban area with a population of at least 200 people to use this ability, and must succeed at a DC 20 Diplomacy check to secure the favor. Once an attempt to call in a favor has been made, whether or not the Diplomacy check was successful, the Hatharat agent must wait 2d6 days before attempting to call in a new favor, so as to not overtax the favors owed the society as a whole.

The Hatharat agent can use a successful called favor to secure an introduction to an important NPC, either to learn an important piece of information (something that would otherwise require a successful DC 20 Knowledge check to know) or to secure a pardon for a minor crime of which an ally has been convicted. Similar favors could be granted at the GM's discretion.

::ab[Informed Persuasion (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost flavor="A Hatharat agent uses his knowledge of a target to increase his persuasiveness." passive="If he has at least 1 rank in an associated Knowledge skill, he applies his Intelligence modifier (in addition to his Charisma modifier, as normal) to Bluff, Diplomacy, and Intimidate checks against that Knowledge skill's associated category of targets." special="The types of Knowledge and their associated groups are arcana (spellcasters), geography (tribal citizens), local (urban citizens), nobility (politicians and nobles), and religion (worshipers of a recognized faith)." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A Hatharat agent gains the bardic performance listed below."}

::ab[Master of Manipulation (Su)]{next icon=power l=8 flavor="A Hatharat agent gains a reputation of being able to sense lies." ability="When using bardic performance, his very presence can often draw the truth out of others. This ability affects all creatures selected by the bard within a 20-foot-radius ‹misc/emanation› from the bard. Any affected creature can't speak any deliberate or intentional lies, but is aware of the effect and can choose to avoid answering a question to which it would normally respond with a lie, or may be evasive as long as it remains within the boundaries of the truth." special="This is a mind-affecting compulsion effect." replace="Dirge of doom"}

hoaxer.name: Hoaxer
description: ## Hoaxer
‹SOURCE Black Markets/29›
Hoaxers specialize in creating valuable-looking counterfeits and infusing these false treasures with dangerous magic to make their marks more vulnerable to future swindles.

::ab[Counterfeiter (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A hoaxer adds half his bard level (minimum 1) on all Appraise, Bluff, and Sleight of Hand checks, as well as on Craft, Knowledge, Linguistics, Perception, and Profession checks to create or detect a counterfeit or forgery. He can attempt such skill checks untrained." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance (Su)]{head icon=power note="A hoaxer dedicates his arcane energies to deceiving others rather than to entertaining them, granting him the following unique performances."}

::ab[Bad Deal (Su)]{next icon=magic flavor="A hoaxer can use his performance to coax a bit of luck (good or bad) into a valuable-seeming object." provokes=Yes hStandard standard="The hoaxer can use 1 round of bardic performance to invest a single object in his possession with a hex he knows. This object must be light enough to hold in one hand, and cannot already have any magical properties. The object remains hexed as long as the hoaxer continues to spend 1 round of bardic performance each round as a ***swift action*** to maintain it, until the hoaxer drops the item, or until triggered. The hex triggers immediately upon being willingly accepted by another creature, targeting its new owner." imp1="The hoaxer knows one ‹ability/witch »hex<es› from the following list: ‹hex/blight›, ‹hex/charm›, ‹hex/evil eye›, ‹hex/fortune›, ‹hex/healing›, ‹hex/misfortune›, ‹hex/slumber›, or ‹hex/unnerve beasts›." imp3="He can learn one additional hex." imp6="He can learn one additional hex." imp9="He can learn one additional hex." imp12="He can learn one additional hex, and the list of hexes grows to include ‹hex/agony›, ‹hex/hoarfrost›, ‹hex/ice tomb›, ‹hex/infected wounds›, ‹hex/nightmares›, ‹hex/retribution›, and ‹hex/speak in dreams›." imp15="He can learn one additional hex." imp18="He can learn one additional hex." special="A bad deal cannot be forced on a creature or slipped into its possession without its knowledge, but a hoaxer can lie or cheat to convince his mark to accept the object, or use spells like *beguiling gift.* The save DC for a hoaxer's hexes is equal to 10 + 1/2 the hoaxer's bard level + his Charisma modifier. These hexes function identically to witch hexes, except they can only target single creatures, and the hoaxer can only use his hexes by investing them into objects. If the hoaxer learns hexes from any other source (such as the ‹class/witch› class), he cannot invoke them into an object." replace="Inspire courage, inspire competence, and inspire greatness"}

::ab[Buyer Beware (Su)]{next icon=magic standard="A hoaxer can convince someone to accept a gift or trade as per ‹spell/beguiling gift› (DC = 10 + 1/2 the hoaxer's bard level + his Charisma modifier) by spending 1 round of bardic performance on a supernatural sales pitch. The hoaxer can use this ability to convince a creature to accept a hexed object created with his bad deal performance." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Personal Guarantee (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="A hoaxer can prevent his hexed wares from afflicting a target until he is safely out of sight. He can spend up to 1 round of bardic performance per bard level while hexing an object. For every round expended, the object's hex is delayed for 1 minute before triggering and hexing its current owner." replace=Distraction}

::ab[Curse Breaker (Su)]{next icon=magic l12="A hoaxer can use performance to create an effect equal to ‹spell/break enchantment›. This otherwise functions as ‹performance/soothing performance›." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Misery (Ex)]{l=2 flavor="A hoaxer learns to take joy in the misfortune of others." icon=boost ability="He gains a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls against creatures suffering from a curse, hex, or harmful mind-affecting effect, as well as a +1 morale bonus on Will saving throws against spells from such creatures." imp5="These bonuses become +2." imp11="These bonuses become +3." imp17="These bonuses become +4." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Versed in Curses (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A hoaxer gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against curses, hexes, and language-dependent effects." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Curse Crafter (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-magic ability="A hoaxer learns how to craft cursed items. He gains one ‹main/item creation feat› as a bonus feat." special="He can use these feats only to craft cursed items. In addition, he can craft items that appear magical but have no true magical properties - as if permanently affected by the ‹spell/magic aura› spell - for 50 gp." imp5="He gains another bonus item creation feat." imp11="He gains another bonus item creation feat." imp17="He gains another bonus item creation feat." replace="Lore master"}

impervious_messenger.name: Impervious Messenger
description: ## Impervious Messenger
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/20›

The impervious messenger is capable of harnessing the unique qualities of bardic magic to keep, transport, and communicate the most precious of secrets and messages, even when subject to the most vigorous forms of observation, be they magical or mundane.
::ab[Bardic Performance (Su)]{head icon=info note="An impervious messenger gains the following types of bardic performances."}

::ab[Chant of Perfect Recall (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="An impervious messenger can spend 1 round of his bardic performance to commit something to memory. This ability functions exactly as per ‹spell/memorize page›, but the impervious messenger is the only recipient and he requires only 1 round to commit a single page to memory. An impervious messenger can commit additional pages worth of material to memory by using additional rounds of his bardic performance, but he can never memorize more than half his total bard level in pages (minimum 1)." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Song of Subterfuge (Su)]{next l=6 icon=magic ability="An impervious messenger learns to use his performances to project various thoughts into his own mind to disrupt divination attempts. This acts similarly to ‹performance/distraction›, except it only affects the impervious messenger and allows him to use the result of his Perform check in place of saving throws against attempts to read his mind, such as ‹spell/detect thoughts›." imp18="If the impervious messenger succeeds on a saving throw while using *song of subterfuge,* the character who attempted the divination effect must succeed on a Will save (DC = 20 + the impervious messenger's Charisma modifier) or else the impervious messenger instantly learns the nature of the effect and can allow the effect to continue, but provide whatever information he chooses to the divining character rather than the true results of the divination effect." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion"}

::ab[Unbroken Stride (Su)]{next l=8 icon=boost ability="An impervious messenger draws upon his bardic abilities to imbue himself with grace and speed. While using this bardic performance, an impervious messenger gains an insight bonus equal to half his bard level on all Acrobatics, Climb, Fly, and Ride checks. In addition, while maintaining this performance, he gains the benefits of the ranger's ‹ability/woodland stride› ability and a +10-foot enhancement bonus to his base land speed." imp12="An impervious messenger using his *unbroken stride* bardic performance also acts as if under the effect of ‹spell/freedom of movement›. In addition, the enhancement bonus to his base land speed increases to +30 feet." replace="Dirge of doom~frightening tune"}

::ab[Cryptic Whisper (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-def flavor="The impervious messenger internalizes his understanding of language and ciphers, and filters them through his bardic abilities." passive="He adds half his bard level to all Linguistics checks and Bluff checks to deliver secret messages, and he gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against ‹main/glyph spells, »symbol spells,› and language-dependent effects." replace="Bardic knowledge~well-versed"}

juggler.name: Juggler
description: ## Juggler
‹SOURCE Ranged Tactics Toolbox/7›

Jugglers are masters of manipulating objects, most famously by keeping multiple objects in the air simultaneously. They often incorporate this talent into acting, comedy, or dance. Adventuring jugglers find their abilities translate well into the use of thrown weapons.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiencies]{icon=info note="A juggler is proficient with all simple weapons, plus martial thrown weapons. A juggler is not proficient with shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Fast Reactions (Ex)]{icon=power l1="A juggler gains ‹feat/Deflect Arrows› as a bonus feat." l5="He gains ‹feat/Snatch Arrows› as a bonus feat." special="The juggler doesn't need to meet the prerequisites for these feats." l11="The juggler can use the ability granted by these feats an additional time each round (with a -5 penalty on his second attack roll)." l17="The juggler gains another use of these feats (with a -10 penalty on his third attack roll)." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}

::ab[Combat Juggling (Ex)]{icon=power l=2 ability="A juggler can hold and wield (in other words, &quot&juggle&quot&) up to three items or weapons in his hands. The juggler must be able to hold and wield an object in one hand in order to juggle it. This ability doesn't grant the juggler additional attacks, though it does allow him to use different weapons as part of a full attack. As long as he is juggling fewer than three objects, the juggler is considered to have a free hand (for the purposes of drawing a weapon, using somatic components, using Deflect Arrows, and so on)." imp6="The juggler can juggle four items or weapons at once." imp10="The juggler can juggle five items or weapons at once." imp14="The juggler can juggle six items or weapons at once." imp18="The juggler can juggle seven items or weapons at once." special="If the juggler is affected by any action or condition that would require a ‹rule/concentration› check while he is juggling, he must attempt a Sleight of Hand check to continue juggling and avoid losing concentration. The check's DC is the same as a concentration check, treating the spell level as twice the number of objects being juggled. If the juggler fails his Sleight of Hand check, he drops all juggled objects but one, determined randomly." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{icon=def l2="A juggler gains ‹ability/evasion›, as the ‹class/rogue› class feature of the same name." l12="He gains ‹talent/improved evasion›, as the advanced rogue talent." replace="Well-versed~soothing performance"}

lotus_geisha.name: Lotus Geisha
description: ## Lotus Geisha
‹SOURCE Dragon Empires Primer/22›
The lotus geisha of Minkai are renowned for their powers of seduction, and tales tell of lotus geisha disarming foes and bolstering allies. The lotus geisha is an archetype of the bard class.

::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=info note="A lotus geisha is proficient with all simple weapons, plus the ‹eq-weapon/butterfly sword›, ‹eq-weapon/fighting fan›, ‹eq-weapon/iron brush›, ‹eq-weapon/sai›, ‹eq-weapon/shortbow›, and ‹eq-weapon/shuriken›." alter="Weapon proficiencies"}

:::ab[Enrapturing Performance (Su)]{l=2 icon=power ability=Ability title="Enrapturing Performance (Su)" hImp imp7="This becomes a ***move action.***" imp13="This becomes a ***swift action.***" replace=Well-versed}

A lotus geisha gains the ability to execute an *enrapturing performance* in addition to her bardic performance ability. An *enrapturing performance* is exactly like a bardic performance with the following exceptions.

A lotus geisha can use an *enrapturing performance* to recreate the effects of any of her known bardic performances, but she focuses the performance on only a single target within range. While other creatures see and hear a lotus geisha's *enrapturing performance,* only the target of this ability is affected by it. A lotus geisha cannot have a bardic performance and an *enrapturing performance* in effect at the same time. Every round spent engaged in an *enrapturing performance* counts against the total number of rounds per day she can use her bardic performance, and she cannot use this ability if she does not have any more rounds of bardic performance left for that day. Starting an *enrapturing performance* is a @HL[standard action].

A lotus geisha adds a +2 bonus to the DC of Will saves made to resist the effects of her *fascinate, frightening tune,* and *suggestion enrapturing performances.* When a lotus geisha uses her *enrapturing performance* ability to emulate *inspire competence, inspire courage, inspire greatness,* or *inspire heroics,* any bonuses to AC or on ability checks, attack rolls, damage rolls, saving throws, and skill checks increase by +1. For example, a lotus geisha using ‹performance/inspire heroics› as an *enrapturing performance* would grant her target a +5 morale bonus on saving throws and a +5 dodge bonus to AC, instead of the usual +4 bonuses.

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l1="A lotus geisha gains ‹feat/Spell Focus› (enchantment) as a bonus feat." l5="She gains ‹feat/Greater Spell Focus› (enchantment) as a bonus feat." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}

luring_piper.name: Luring Piper
description: ## Luring Piper
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/11›

Some bards have an exceptional ability to use their musical performances to entrance the world's wilder creatures. These performers are so focused that they create melodies capable of leading animals and fey to their unwilling death.
::ab[Class Skills]{icon=skill note="A luring piper gains Survival as a class skill." alter="The bard's class skills"}

::ab[Fey Secrets]{icon=boost passive="A luring piper adds half his class level (minimum 1) on Bluff, Knowledge (nature), Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks when interacting with fey." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Luring Presentation (Ex)]{icon=lower-down passive="Creatures that have the animal or fey type take a -2 penalty on saving throws imposed by a luring piper's bardic performances. Creatures with any other type gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against a luring piper's bardic performances." alter="Bardic performance"}

::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="The luring piper gains the following types of bardic performances that can only be made when using Perform (wind) and a when employing a wind instrument."}

::ab[Charming Melody (Su)]{next icon=power ability="While using the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance, the luring piper can play a song that compels animals and fey to follow him wherever he goes. Instead of sitting peacefully and merely observing the luring piper, creatures with the animal or fey type instead quietly and calmly walk toward the luring piper, stopping once they are adjacent to the luring piper and following him if he moves." alter=Fascinate}

::ab[Deadly Lure (Sp)]{next l=8 icon=boost ability="The luring piper can use his ‹performance/suggestion› bardic performance on targeted animals and fey to make them take actions that result in their own injury or death, such as leaping off a cliff or into a river. The targeted creature is allowed a second saving throw to shake off the effect before completing any self-harming actions." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Fey-Wounding Song (Sp)]{next icon=power l=12 fullround="The luring piper can expend 3 rounds of bardic performance to play a song that causes fey tremendous pain. Treat this as equivalent to ‹spell/mass inflict serious wounds›, but affecting only fey targets and using the lurid piper's bard level as his caster level." replace="Soothing performance"}

::ab[Piper's Attention (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def-roll passive="Whenever a luring piper is actively using Perform (wind) as part of a bardic performance, he gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against language-dependent, mind-affecting, and sonic effects. If this effect is caused by a fey creature, the luring piper can also roll the saving throw twice and take the better result." replace=Well-versed}

magician.name: Magician
description: ## Magician
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/82›
A magician dabbles in performance, but sees it as a means to tap into universal energies and channel them.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A magician gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Dweomercraft (Su)]{next icon=boost ability="A magician can use performance to manipulate magical energies. Allies of the magician gain a +1 bonus on caster level checks, ‹rule/concentration› checks, and attack rolls with spells and spell-like abilities." imp5="This bonus becomes +2." imp11="This bonus becomes +3." imp17="This bonus becomes +4." special="This ability relies on visual and audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Spell Suppression (Su)]{next icon=magic l=8 flavor="A magician can use performance to counter the spells of his foes." hAbility ability="Once the bard begins using this performance, he tracks the number of rounds it has been in use. While performing, as an ***immediate action,*** he can attempt to counter any spell that he can identify using Spellcraft, so long as that spell's level is equal or less than the total number of rounds he has been performing spell suppression. The attempt to counter the spell is made as if using ‹spell/dispel magic›, using the bard's level as the caster level. If successful, the bardic performance immediately ends." special="This ability requires audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Metamagic Mastery (Ex)]{next icon=magic-boost l=14 ability="A magician can use performance to apply a ‹main/metamagic feat› to a spell he is about to cast without increasing the casting time. The bard must still expend a higher-level slot to cast this spell. This causes the performance to immediately end." special="This ability requires audible components." replace="Frightening tune"}
::ab[Magical Talent (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A magician gains a bonus equal to half his level on Knowledge (arcana), Spellcraft, and Use Magic Device checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Improved Counterspell]{icon=power ability="A magician gains ‹feat/Improved Counterspell› as a bonus feat." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Extended Performance (Su)]{l=2 icon=boost swift="A magician can extend the duration of bardic performance after he stops concentrating by sacrificing a spell slot. The performance effect lingers for 1 extra round per level of the spell." special="Only one spell may be sacrificed per performance, and performance types that take affect after a specific number of rounds cannot be extended." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Expanded Repertoire (Ex)]{icon=learn l2="A magician can add one spell to his spells known from the spell list of any arcane spellcasting class. The spell must be of a level he can cast." l6="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." l10="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." l14="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." l18="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Arcane Bond (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A magician gains the *arcane bond* ability as a ‹class/wizard›. He may not choose a familiar or a weapon as a bonded item." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Wand Mastery (Ex)]{icon=boost l10="When a magician uses a wand containing a spell on his spell list, he uses his Charisma bonus to set the wand's savel" imp16="When using such a wand, he uses his caster level in place of the wand's caster level." replace="Jack of all trades"}

masked_performer.name: Masked Performer
description: ## Masked Performer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/20›

Not merely an entertainer in a disguise, the masked performer takes on the persona represented by the likeness she wears. Her alter ego might be a theatrical archetype, a legendary hero, or a revered spirit. Masked performers are often the stars of religious plays and festivals, and aficionados easily recognize them and associate them with their most famous role.

::ab[Dual Identity (Ex)]{icon=power ability="This ability acts as the ‹class/vigilante› ability of the same name, except as follows. The masked performer's identities are referred to as social and masked, rather than social and vigilante. Unlike a vigilante, most people who know of the masked performer actually know that the masked performer's identities are one and the same. Nonetheless, the dual identity ability is valuable to the masked performer due to offering her two perspectives on any problem and two different alignments." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A masked performer gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Seamless Guise (Ex)]{next icon=power-boost ability="A masked performer learns to behave in a way that appears perfectly proper and normal for her current role, on stage or off. She can maintain this performance for 1 hour for every 3 rounds of bardic performance she spends. While this performance is active, she receives a +10 bonus on Disguise and Perform (act) checks to remain in character after she has already established her character. For instance, this bonus does not apply against the initial Perception check when meeting a creature paying attention to her but does apply on the automatic additional Perception check each hour of interacting." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Exaggerated Pose (Su)]{next icon=boost l=3 order=aw ability="A masked performer can enhance her own physical and social abilities by simply altering her posture. When she starts this performance, the masked performer chooses a Strength-, Dexterity-, or Charisma-based skill and gains a +2 competence bonus on checks with that skill as long as she maintains this performance." imp7="The bonus becomes +3." imp11="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="This bonus becomes +5." imp19="The bonus becomes +6." swift="The masked performer can change the chosen skill." special="*Exaggerated pose* is an emotion effect and relies on visual components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Stage Combat (Su)]{next icon=power l=6 ability="A masked performer gains the benefit of a ‹main/combat feat› she doesn't have as long as she maintains this performance. The masked performer chooses the feat when she starts this performance. The masked performer must meet all the feat's prerequisites." special="As usual when temporarily gaining a feat, if the feat she chooses has any limitations on uses, such as ‹feat/Stunning Fist«'s› daily uses, all uses of that combat feat while maintaining *stage combat* count toward that feat's limit. In the case of Stunning Fist, if the masked performer gained that feat again later the same day, the expended uses would remain expended. *Stage combat* relies on visual components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Multiplicity of Masks (Su)]{next icon=magic-aura l=18 ability="A masked performer can cause any number of her allies to appear as herself or as another ally within 60 feet. To be affected, an ally must be within 60 feet of the masker performer and able to see her. As long as the masked performer maintains this performance, enemies perceive all affected allies as the chosen character. If an affected ally is a different size or creature type than the chosen character, enemies can attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the masked performer's bard level + her Charisma modifier) to disbelieve. Otherwise, there is no saving throw, but a successful attack against a disguised ally automatically reveals the ally's true form." special="If an ally moves out of range, that ally reverts to his original form, and the entire effect ends if the ally providing his appearance to the others moves out of range. *Multiplicity of masks* is an illusion (glamer) effect that relies on visual components." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Social Grace (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A masked performer gains the ‹soctalent/social grace› vigilante social talent." special="She gains an additional skill with *social grace* 1 level later than a vigilante (at 6th level, 10th level, 14th level, and 18th level)." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Masked Talents]{icon=power l5="A masked performer gains a rogue talent from the following list: ‹talent/expert leaper›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/ledge walker›, ‹talent/rogue crawl›, ‹talent/rope master›, ‹talent/stand up›, and ‹talent/wall scramble›." l17="A masked performer can choose any of the above talents or an advanced rogue talent from the following list: ‹talent/defensive roll›, ‹talent/fast tumble›, ‹talent/hide in plain sight›, and ‹talent/skill mastery›." special="This ability does not qualify her for the ‹feat/Extra Rogue Talent› feat." replace="Lore master"}

mute_musician.name: Mute Musician
description: ## Mute Musician
‹SOURCE Horror Realms/9›

A mute musician forswears speech for the unnatural songs and thunderous silences of the depths of space. She learns to blend light and sound in seemingly impossible ways. Some mute musicians look to the darker aspects of ‹faith/Desna› as their inspiration for these performances, but most mute musicians find their muse in the alien sounds of the Elder Mythos and the entities that orbit the blind idiot god ‹faith/Azathoth›, who floats in the heart of the Dark Tapestry.

::ab[Mute (Ex)]{icon=down ability="A mute musician has a disability or injury (possibly self-inflicted or even psychological) that prevents her from speaking or vocalizing. Language-dependent effects (including Perform &#91&oratory&#93&) require the bard to be able to use telepathy, nonverbal languages, or writing to communicate. She cannot use Perform (sing) or speak languages, though she can still create audible bardic performances by means of a musical instrument. A mute musician can provide verbal and somatic components for spells she casts via any musical instrument in which she has ranks in the appropriate Perform skill."}
::ab[Eschew Materials (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A mute musician gains ‹feat/Eschew Materials› as a bonus feat at 1st level." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A mute musician gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Symphony of Silence (Su)]{next icon=aura-protect l=3 ability="The mute musician's music muffles all other sounds within a 30-foot radius. All creatures in the area of effect gain a +2 bonus on all saving throws made against sonic attacks or language-dependent effects." imp7="The bonus becomes +3." imp11="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp19="The bonus becomes +6." special="Symphony of silence relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Maddening Harmonics (Su)]{next icon=aura-lower l=14 ability="The mute musician can create a performance so baffling and discordant that it usurps all thought within a 30-foot radius with chaos and entropy. The mute musician can select which creatures in this area are affected by the maddening harmonics and which are not. All targeted creatures within this area must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) or be confused for as long as they can hear the performance." special="If the creature succeeds at its saving throw, it is immune to this ability for 24 hours. This performance relies on audible components." replace="Frightening tune"}

::ab[Ceaseless Performance (Su)]{next icon=power hAbility l=15 ability="The mute musician can continue taking the ***free action*** to maintain a bardic performance even while confused, cowering in fear, dazed, nauseated, panicked, paralyzed, petrified, silenced, staggered, stunned, or unconscious. Even if the mute musician is killed, she can continue to take the free action to maintain her performance as long as she has rounds remaining. Only the utter destruction of the mute musician's body (such as via ‹spell/destruction› or ‹spell/disintegrate›, or by reducing the bard to a negative hit point total equal to 10 &times& her Constitution score) causes the performance to end." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Song of the Conjunction (Su)]{next icon=magic l=18 ability="The mute musician can harmonize with the alien chorus beyond the music of the spheres, creating a portentous cosmic alignment. This effect duplicates a ‹spell/gate› used to travel, save that the destination point must be on the same plane as the bard, but it is not otherwise limited by distance." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Insights from Beyond (Ex)]{l=2 icon=learn choice="A mute musician adds two abjuration, conjuration (calling), conjuration (summoning), or conjuration (teleportation) spells from the ‹main/spells_>sorcerer›/‹main/spells_>wizard› spell list to her list of bard spells known." imp6="The bard can choose two more spells to add to her spells known." imp10="The bard can choose two more spells to know." imp14="The bard can choose two more spells to know." imp18="The bard can choose two more spells to know." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Dulled Horror (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="At 2nd level, a mute musician gains a +4 bonus on saves against confusion, fear, insanity effects, and the supernatural abilities of aberrations." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Eldritch Caesura (Su)]{l=10 icon=boost ability="A mute musician can insert unsettling silences into her otherworldly music by spending an additional 1 round of bardic performance per round. This supernatural technique impossibly blurs the line between music and light, transmitting audible performances and sonic bard spells through most barriers save lead. This allows the music and sonic spells to affect creatures across planar boundaries (including on the ‹rule/Ethereal<_plane› or ‹rule/Shadow Plane«s›), vacuums, and areas of magical silence." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}

::ab[Ex-Mute Musicians]{icon=warning note="A mute musician who regains the ability to speak or chooses to speak aloud loses all abilities granted by this archetype. She can regain the archetype's abilities by spending 24 hours without speaking."}

negotiator.name: Negotiator
description: ## Negotiator
‹SOURCE Champions of Balance/18›
Bards are experts at manipulating the emotions and perceptions of others. Negotiators are those bards who take the skills of a performer and apply them to the realms of business and law.
::ab[Hard Bargainer]{icon=boost passive="A negotiator adds half his class level (minimum 1) on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, Knowledge (local), and Sense Motive checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A negotiator gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Counterargument (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="This performance acts as ‹performance/countersong›, except a negotiator can use only Perform (act, comedy, oratory, or sing) to perform a counterargument."}

::ab[Fast Talk (Su)]{next icon=lower l=1 ability="A negotiator can use performance to affect the perceptions of any number of targets that can hear him. While the negotiator performs, these targets take a -1 penalty on saving throws against enchantment (charm) and illusion (figment, glamer, or shadow) effects, as well as a penalty on Appraise checks equal to half the negotiator's level (minimum -1). A target that fails its Appraise check believes that the object in question is worth 10% more or less (negotiator's choice) than its actual value." imp5="The penalty on saving throws becomes -2 and the percentage becomes 20%." imp11="These become -3 and 30%." imp17="These become -4 and 40%." special="Fast talk is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that uses audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Binding Contract (Sp)]{next l=9 icon=magic ability="The negotiator can bind another creature's promise to the negotiator with a geas. This acts as ‹spell/lesser geas›, except the creature must have Hit Dice equal to or less than the negotiator's level. The target can negate this effect with a successful Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the negotiator's level + the negotiator's Charisma modifier). The geas is discharged if the negotiator breaks his end of the bargain. Using this ability requires 3 rounds of continuous performance, and the target must be able to see and hear the bard throughout the performance." imp18="This effect acts as ‹spell/geas/quest› (no HD limit, and the target cannot attempt a saving throw)." special="Binding contract is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting, language-dependent ability that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Rogue Talents]{icon=power l2="A negotiator gains a ‹ability/rogue talent›, as the rogue class feature of the same name." l6="The negotiator gains another talent." l10="The negotiator gains another talent." l14="The negotiator gains another talent." l18="The negotiator gains another talent." special="A negotiator cannot select a rogue talent that modifies the sneak attack ability." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Master of Rhetoric (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="The negotiator can take 10 on any Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, Knowledge (local), or Sense Motive check if he has ranks in that skill. A negotiator can choose not to take 10 and can instead roll normally. In addition, once per day, the negotiator can take 20 on one of those skill checks (without spending any additional time)." imp11="He can take 20 twice a day." imp17="He can take 20 three times a day." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Advanced Talents]{icon=boost l10="A negotiator can now select an ‹ability/advanced rogue talent› in place of a rogue talent. This also applies to the talents gained at 14th and 18th levels." special="A negotiator cannot select an advanced talent that modifies the sneak attack ability."}

phrenologist.name: Phrenologist
description: ## Phrenologist
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/114›
The phrenologist is an expert at reading creatures' skulls, and he learns to use this knowledge to his benefit.

::ab[Phrenological Knowledge (Ex)]{icon=power-boost ability="A phrenologist is an expert at reading creatures' skulls. He gains ‹feat/Psychic Sensitivity› as a bonus feat, and he can use the phrenology skill unlock even if he is untrained in ‹skill/Knowledge (arcana)›." order=ap passive="He adds half his class level (minimum 1) on all Knowledge (arcana) checks for the phrenology occult skill unlock." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Skull-Versed (Ex)]{icon=lower l=2 ability="A phrenologist can use knowledge gleaned from a creature's skull to tailor a performance. All creatures on which the phrenologist has successfully used his phrenology skill unlock take a -2 penalty on all saving throws against the phrenologist's bardic performances." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Phrenological Savant]{icon=boost l=10 ability="A phrenologist gains either ‹feat/Psychic Maestro› or ‹feat/Psychic Virtuoso› as a bonus feat. If he chooses Psychic Maestro, he must select phrenology as one of his two skill unlocks to use an additional time." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A phrenologist gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Skull Sonata (Su)]{next icon=aura ability="A phrenologist learns to use the resonance of other creatures' skulls to increase sonic damage. All enemies within 30 feet of the phrenologist that have skulls take an amount of additional sonic damage equal to half the phrenologist's bard level (minimum 1) each time they take sonic damage." special="*Skull sonata* relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[In Your Head (Sp)]{next icon=magic l3="A phrenologist can use his performance to send his own senses into another's skull, as the spell ‹spell/witness› (DC = 10 + 1/2 the phrenologist's class level + his Charisma modifier)." l11="This performance's duration increases to 10 minutes per bard level." l19="The duration increases to 1 hour per bard level." special="*In your head* relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Fingers of Fascination (Su)]{next icon=power ability="When a phrenologist uses the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance, he can send forth psychic fingers along with his performance, allowing him to study one of the fascinated creatures with his phrenology skill unlock without touching its head physically and without it being helpless, willing, or paralyzed. This does not spend his daily use of the phrenology skill unlock." alter=Fascinate}

pitax_academy_of_grand_arts.name: Pitax: Academy of Grand Arts
description: ## Pitax: Academy of Grand Arts
‹SOURCE Guide to the River Kingdoms/40›
